title: cinnamomum grandiflorum
intro: Cinnamomum grandiflorum, also known as Sandalwood or East Indian Sandalwood, is a slow-growing, evergreen tree native to India and Southeast Asia. It is valued for its fragrant heartwood, which contains a number of medicinal compounds including alpha-santalol and beta-santalol, known for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The oil extracted from the wood has been used to treat various health issues, including anxiety, insomnia, and skin conditions, due to its calming and soothing effects. Sandalwood oil can be prepared through steam distillation and is often used in aromatherapy, skincare products, and traditional Ayurvedic medicine.
